seoTitle: Darling Point vs Darlinghurst — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Darling Point and Darlinghurst. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Darling Point vs Darlinghurst: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Darling Point and Darlinghurst.
heading: Darling Point vs Darlinghurst — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Darling Point, New South Wales and Darlinghurst, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Darling Point has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Darlinghurst (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Darling Point or Darlinghurst safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Darling Point has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Darlinghurst scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Darling Point scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Darlinghurst scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Darling Point or Darlinghurst?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Darling Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.38% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
